title: anthurium subcarinatum
intro: Anthurium subcarinatum, also known as the subcarinate anthurium, is a species of flowering plant in the arum family (Araceae). This plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, where it grows as a terrestrial or epiphytic herb. Anthurium subcarinatum has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in herbal remedies, such as salves and ointments, to take advantage of its health benefits, including wound healing, pain relief, and antiseptic properties.
